title: Exotic Yeasty Kenyan Dough Flatbread
description: This traditional Kenyan flatbread is made with a long-fermentation dough and features tamarind pulp, making it a unique and delicious crowd-pleaser.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the flour, yeast, salt, sugar, turmeric, and ginger.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the yogurt, tamarind pulp, and water.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it ferment in a warm place for 4 hours.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Divide the dough into 6 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Brush the tops with olive oil and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
